content:
Recently I’ve gathered the motivation to actually make a webcomic/novel/long format narrative. One of the roadblocks I’ll have to tackle is my disdain for selfinserst. I’ve always thought they were cheap and an attempt for the author to live their power fantasy. But after reading the after word on Welcome to the NHK I realized they’re kinda unavoidable. In the afterword it says that the author was himself a dropout shut-in. He said it was painful at times to write it because it dealt with problems he himself dealth with, and that he had trouble separating himself from the characters, world and events that he set out to write. After reading that I realized self-inserts are kinda unavoidable. For one you’d want to draw characters and stories that resonate with your own life experience, ie talking about topics that one has dealth with and from one’s very own and personal perspective. This also makes sense from the perspective that one should only write was one knows and is “qualified” to talk about. A novel about a depressed shut-in written by an extroverted life enjoyer would feel cheap. Your own life experience is your qualification in a way.

Also there’s the fact that one’s art is undoubtedly tainted by one’s hand, it’s a fingerprint. And by extension the characters are too.
